title: Fans boo, players adapt - the view on World Cup hydration breaks
summary: Mid-half hydration breaks at the World Cup generate mixed reactions, with fans booing while coaches and players adapt to the new format. The breaks represent an evolving aspect of modern World Cup play.
content: Supporters are booing them, coaches are using them, players are adapting to them - mid-half hydration breaks are affecting everyone at the World Cup in different ways.

summary: Colombia secured a World Cup victory over first-time participants Uzbekistan, with Diaz scoring a pivotal goal. The win marked Colombia's triumphant return to the tournament.

title: Messi, Mbappe & Vinicius Jr - best World Cup goals from round one

title: Fayzullaev scores Uzbekistan's first-ever World Cup goal
summary: Uzbekistan achieved a historic milestone as midfielder Abbosbek Fayzullaev scored his nation's first-ever World Cup goal, an important equalizer against Colombia. The achievement marks a significant moment for the debutant nation.
content: Abbosbek Fayzullaev scores Uzbekistan's first-ever goal at a World Cup final, tapping in an equaliser against Colombia.

title: What are the patches on some shirts at the World Cup?
summary: Several players, including England captain Harry Kane, wear special patches on their World Cup shirts for various purposes and eligibility reasons. The article explains what these distinctive modifications represent and who wears them.

title: Ghana score late winner to break Panama hearts
summary: Ghana's Caleb Yirenkki scored a dramatic 95th-minute winner against Panama in Toronto, denying the Central American team their first-ever World Cup point. The late goal delivered a heartbreaking loss for Panama.
content: Ghana's Caleb Yirenkyi scored a 95th-minute winner to deny Panama their first-ever World Cup point in Toronto, Canada.

zh.title: 加纳打入绝杀球击碎巴拿马的希望

title: Are goalkeepers struggling with the World Cup ball?
summary: Pundits Joe Hart, Wayne Rooney, and Mark Chapman discuss whether goalkeepers are struggling with the official World Cup ball following several long-range goals in early matches. The conversation examines whether equipment is impacting goalkeeper performance.
content: Joe Hart, Wayne Rooney and Mark Chapman discuss whether goalkeepers are struggling to get to grips with the World Cup ball, after a number of long range goals.

zh.title: 门将们在世界杯用球上遇到困难吗？

title: This was an England we haven't seen for years - and it was fun
summary: Phil McNulty praises England's entertaining attacking football and intent in a recent match, noting it represents a refreshing style the team hasn't displayed in years. The performance demonstrates the team's potential under their current setup.
content: Fun to watch and full of intent - this was an England we have not seen for years, writes Phil McNulty.

zh.title: 这是多年未见的英格兰 - 而且很有趣

title: 'Chip on shoulder' may help Bellingham to best form
summary: Jude Bellingham believes external scrutiny about his role in the England team may motivate him to reach peak form during the World Cup. The midfielder suggests the pressure could work in his favor as he aims to deliver at the tournament.
content: Jude Bellingham says the external "noise" around his place in the England team may help him find his best form during the World Cup.

zh.title: 心有不甘可能帮助贝林厄姆达到最佳状态
zh.summary: 朱德·贝林厄姆相信外界对他在英格兰队中角色的关注可能会激励他在世界杯期间达到最佳状态。这位中场球员认为压力可能对他有利，因为他的目标是在这项赛事中有所表现。

title: Drone brought down near South Korea training base
summary: Mexican military intercepted and brought down an unregistered drone flying over South Korea's World Cup training base before their Group A match. The incident highlights security concerns during the tournament.
content: The Mexican military reportedly intercepts and brings down an unregistered drone seen flying over South Korea's training base before their Group A match with co-hosts Mexico on Friday.

zh.title: 无人机在韩国训练基地附近被击落
zh.summary: 墨西哥军方拦截并击落了在韩国世界杯训练基地上空飞行的一架未注册无人机，事件发生在他们的小组赛比赛前。该事件突显了锦标赛期间的安全问题。
